title: Medicine Gave Women the Wrong Hormone for Decades
description: The 2002 Women's Health Initiative study that scared millions of women off hormone therapy didn't test natural progesterone — it tested a synthetic drug with a fundamentally different molecular profile. The distinction between bioidentical progesterone and synthetic progestins is not a fringe alternative-medicine claim; it's a pharmacological reality that mainstream medicine was slow to act on, and the consequences were measurable. This piece investigates how the conflation happened, what the evidence actually shows about each compound's risk profile, and what the correction looks like twenty years later.
